context: 古装宫廷剧中妃嫔对皇帝的自称，表达顺从和谦卑

en.principle: 保留妃嫔身份的尊贵感和对皇帝的顺从语气。用第一人称+敬语结构，不要用第三人称或仆人类词汇
en.cultural_note: In Chinese palace dramas, 'chen qie' is a humble self-reference used by imperial consorts when addressing the Emperor. It conveys deference and a complex mix of submission and dignity — never servitude.

context: 皇后或高位妃嫔的自称，带有权威和身份感

en.principle: 本宫是身份宣示，不是地点。翻译时体现说话者的权威感。普通对话用 I 即可，强调身份时用 This Empress/This Consort
en.cultural_note: 'Ben Gong' literally means 'this palace' but is used as a first-person pronoun by high-ranking consorts, asserting their status and authority within the imperial court.

context: 对皇帝的尊称

en.principle: 直接用 Your Majesty。注意 Your Highness 是王子/公主用的，不是皇帝
en.cultural_note: The formal address for the Emperor in Chinese court. Equivalent to 'Your Majesty' in European royal courts.

context: 太监或低等级宫人对皇帝/主子的自称，清朝特有

en.principle: 奴才在清宫剧中是固定自称，不带现代'奴隶'的负面含义。翻译时保留谦卑但避免 slave 一词的种族敏感性
en.cultural_note: 'Nu cai' was a self-deprecating term used by eunuchs and servants in the Qing Dynasty court. Despite literally meaning 'slave', it was a standard form of address showing loyalty, not actual enslavement.

context: 大臣对皇帝的自称，谦虚但有一定地位

en.principle: 微臣是谦称但说话者是有实权的官员。翻译要体现谦虚但不卑微
en.cultural_note: A humble self-reference used by court officials when addressing the Emperor. The speaker typically holds significant power despite the self-deprecating language.

context: 太后的自称，带有丧夫的悲凉和至高权威

en.principle: 哀家字面是'悲哀之家'，但在剧中是太后的权威自称。翻译时体现权威而非悲伤，除非剧情需要
en.cultural_note: 'Ai jia' literally means 'this grieving one' — a self-reference by the Empress Dowager acknowledging her widowhood. In practice, it signals supreme authority in the inner court.

context: 又爱又恨的人，宫斗剧中常用于情敌或命中注定的对手

en.principle: 冤家在中文里带有宿命感和爱恨交织的复杂情感。根据语境选择偏爱还是偏恨的翻译
en.cultural_note: 'Yuan jia' describes someone with whom you share a complicated, fateful bond — part rival, part kindred spirit. It implies destiny brought you together, whether you like it or not.

context: 皇帝选择当晚侍寝妃嫔的宫廷仪式

good: choose a consort for the night
en.principle: 这是古代宫廷特有的制度，需要解释性翻译。直译'翻牌子'毫无意义
en.cultural_note: A Qing Dynasty palace ritual where the Emperor would flip over a jade tablet bearing a consort's name to summon her for the night. It was a highly formalized and politically significant act.

context: 皇帝将失宠妃嫔囚禁在冷僻宫殿，是严厉惩罚

good: cast into exile within the palace walls
en.principle: 冷宫是专有名词概念，保留 Cold Palace 大写。强调这是终身幽禁式的惩罚
en.cultural_note: The 'Cold Palace' was a remote, neglected section of the Forbidden City where disgraced consorts were effectively imprisoned for life — stripped of rank, visitors, and hope of restoration.

context: 皇帝下令某人自尽，被视为一种'恩赐'

good: ordered to take their own life
en.principle: 赐死在古代是'恩赐'——让罪人体面地死去而非公开处刑。翻译时保留这层讽刺感
en.cultural_note: 'Ci si' — the Emperor 'granting' death — was considered a form of mercy in imperial China, allowing the condemned to die privately rather than face public execution. The cruel irony is intentional.

context: 皇帝选妃的宫廷选拔仪式，非现代选秀节目

en.principle: 古代选秀是政治婚姻制度，不是娱乐节目。避免任何现代选秀节目的联想
en.cultural_note: The Imperial Selection was a formal process where eligible young women from noble families were brought to the palace for the Emperor to choose consorts — a political arrangement, not a beauty contest.

context: 正式授予妃嫔封号和位分

good: formally elevate to the rank of...
en.principle: 册封是重大宫廷仪式，翻译要体现庄严和正式感
en.cultural_note: An elaborate court ceremony conferring official rank and title upon a consort, complete with edicts, ceremonial robes, and formal proclamation — a pivotal moment in palace politics.

context: 比喻在强者面前冒犯，自不量力

good: you dare provoke someone far above your station?
how bold of you to challenge your betters
en.principle: 意译为主，传达'在强者面前挑衅'的含义。不要翻译太岁的字面意思
en.cultural_note: Tai Sui is a deity in Chinese folk religion associated with the planet Jupiter. 'Disturbing the earth above Tai Sui' means provoking someone far more powerful than yourself — a very dangerous act.

concept: 三十年河东三十年河西
context: 风水轮流转，今日得意者明日可能落魄

good: fortune is a wheel that never stops turning
today's victor may be tomorrow's fallen
the tides of fate spare no one
bad: thirty years east river thirty years west river
en.principle: 这是经典哲理台词，翻译要有格言感和节奏感。完全意译
en.cultural_note: A proverb about the impermanence of fortune, derived from the Yellow River's habit of changing course over decades. It warns the powerful not to be complacent.

context: 你可以看到一个人的外表但无法看透内心

good: you can know someone's face but never their heart
trust no one at face value
bad: know person know face not know heart
en.principle: 宫斗剧经典台词，翻译要有阴谋感和警告语气
en.cultural_note: A common saying in palace intrigue dramas, warning that allies and enemies may be indistinguishable behind their courtly masks.

context: 对皇后或高位妃嫔的尊称

en.principle: 娘娘是宫廷敬称，不是'妈妈'。根据被称呼者的位分选择 Your Grace 或 Empress
en.cultural_note: 'Niang niang' is a respectful form of address for high-ranking women in the imperial palace — similar to 'Your Grace' or 'my Lady' in European court protocol.

context: 宫廷中向上位者行礼问好的仪式

en.principle: 请安是宫廷日常礼仪，翻译时体现仪式感。不同场合的请安有不同正式程度
en.cultural_note: A daily court ritual where lower-ranking members formally greet their superiors, typically with specific postures — kneeling for the Emperor, curtseying for consorts.

context: 妃嫔被选中陪伴皇帝过夜

good: spend the night with His Majesty
en.principle: 含蓄处理，保留宫廷语境的委婉和正式感。不需要露骨表达
en.cultural_note: A formal euphemism for a consort being chosen to spend the night with the Emperor — an event of enormous political significance in palace dynamics.

context: 皇后或高位妃嫔的正式礼服，象征至高地位

good: the phoenix crown and ceremonial robes
en.principle: 凤冠霞帔是中国最高等级的女性礼服，翻译要体现庄严和奢华
en.cultural_note: The phoenix crown (feng guan) and cloud-patterned ceremonial cape (xia pei) represent the highest honor for women in imperial China — worn only at coronations, weddings, and the most important state ceremonies.

context: 不要看不起年轻人现在的贫穷，未来不可限量

good: never underestimate a young man with nothing to lose
mock me now — I will have the last laugh
en.principle: 这是热血逆袭名言，翻译要有气势和戏剧张力。常出现在角色受辱后的独白
en.cultural_note: A beloved underdog declaration in Chinese storytelling — the hero vows that those who look down on them now will regret it when they rise to power.
